input.plan:
# Cap roles per company (progressive disclosure)

## Problem

[`CompanyCard.jsx`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx) renders **every** open job when the company is expanded:

```307:316:frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx
      <div className="positions">
        {!isCollapsed && openJobs.length ? (
          openJobs.map((job) => (
            <JobCard
              key={job.idempotency_key || job.url}
              job={job}
              company={company}
              variant="open"
            />
```

Each [`position-card`](relocation_jobs/static/js/position-card.js) is tall (title, meta, action row). On mobile, a company with 20–40 roles becomes a long scroll tunnel before the next company appears. Full company collapse (“Hide positions”) exists but is all-or-nothing — it does not help when you want a quick scan of a few roles.

## UX recommendation (chosen)

**Progressive disclosure with a preview + “Show N more”**, not nested scrolling and not auto-collapsing companies.

| Pattern | Verdict |
|---------|---------|
| Preview N roles + “Show N more / Show less” | **Use** — Oracle Alta “More link”, mobile accordion guidance (keep panels to ~3–5 items), matches existing [`CITY_PREVIEW_LIMIT = 3`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx) and FetchPanel preview |
| Nested `max-height` + scroll inside the card | Avoid — scroll chaining / trapped scroll is a known mobile anti-pattern |
| Default-collapse companies with many roles | Avoid as primary fix — extra tap on every large company; collapse already exists for full hide |
| Ungroup into a flat job list | Out of scope — board is company-centric by design ([`docs/reference/board.md`](docs/reference/board.md)) |

**Defaults:**
- Preview **5** open roles (after existing `sortJobsForDisplay`: ATS-scored → pinned → rest)
- Control copy: `Show 12 more roles` / `Show less`
- Apply at **all breakpoints** when `openJobs.length > 5` (same trap exists on desktop for large employers; one behavior is simpler)
- Same preview for rejected / not-for-me sections when those are toggled on
- Expanded state is **local to the card** (reset on remount / page change) — no `localStorage` unless you later want it

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  company[CompanyCard expanded]
  sorted[sortJobsForDisplay]
  preview{roles greater than 5?}
  show5[Render first 5 JobCards]
  showAll[Render all JobCards]
  moreBtn["Show N more roles"]
  lessBtn[Show less]

  company --> sorted --> preview
  preview -->|no| showAll
  preview -->|yes and collapsed preview| show5 --> moreBtn
  moreBtn -->|tap| showAll --> lessBtn
  lessBtn -->|tap| show5
```

## Implementation

Single meaningful change surface: [`frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx) (+ light CSS).

1. Add `ROLE_PREVIEW_LIMIT = 5` and local state `rolesExpanded` (parallel to `citiesExpanded`).
2. Slice `openJobs` (and rejected / not-for-me lists) unless expanded; render a full-width button under the list using the existing `.expand-cities-btn` visual language (or a sibling class `.expand-roles-btn` for spacing).
3. Keep the existing company-level collapse untouched: collapsed company still shows no roles; preview only applies when positions are visible.
4. CSS in [`relocation_jobs/static/styles.css`](relocation_jobs/static/styles.css): make the control easy to hit on coarse pointers (`min-height: 2.5rem` under the existing `@media (max-width: 720px)` / coarse block). Optional polish: sticky `.company-header` on mobile while scrolling an expanded long list (only if it does not fight the page sticky toolbar — skip if awkward).
5. Rebuild frontend (`cd frontend && npm run build`) so the panel static bundle picks it up.

No API / pagination changes — this is client-side display only on data already returned per company.

## Out of scope

- Changing board company pagination or server payload shape
- Redesigning `position-card` density (would help further but is a separate pass)
- Persisting per-company expand state across sessions

todos.content: Add ROLE_PREVIEW_LIMIT + rolesExpanded state and slice open/rejected/not-for-me lists in CompanyCard.jsx
Add Show N more / Show less control styled for mobile touch targets
Rebuild frontend bundle so panel serves the updated CompanyCard

# Cap roles per company on mobile (progressive disclosure)

## Problem

[`CompanyCard.jsx`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx) renders **every** open job when the company is expanded. Each `position-card` is tall; on mobile a company with dozens of roles becomes a scroll tunnel before the next company.

## UX-skills check (method validation)

Reviewed against [`.claude/ux-skills/apple-design`](.claude/ux-skills/apple-design/SKILL.md) and [`.claude/ux-skills/emil-design-eng`](.claude/ux-skills/emil-design-eng/SKILL.md).

| Principle | Implication for this fix |
|-----------|--------------------------|
| **Simplicity** (Apple §16.6) — common path first, advanced one level deeper | Preview a few roles; full list only on explicit “Show N more” |
| **Flexibility** (Apple §16.5) — phone = quick touch; desktop = deep workflows | **Mobile-only preview** (`max-width: 720px`). Desktop keeps the full list |
| **Agency** (Apple §16.2) — user stays in control | Expand/collapse is user-driven; do not auto-collapse companies |
| **Familiarity** (Apple §16.4) — same pattern already in product | Mirror [`CITY_PREVIEW_LIMIT`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx) “+N more / Show less” |
| **Specific labels** (Apple §16.8) | `Show 12 more roles` / `Show less` — not generic “More” |
| **Wayfinding + spatial consistency** (Apple §7, §16) | On **Show less**, scroll the company card into view so the user is not stranded mid-list |
| **Response / press feedback** (Apple §1, Emil buttons) | Control gets instant `:active { transform: scale(0.97) }` |
| **Motion frequency** (Emil) — list expand is occasional | Light feedback OK; **do not animate `height` of dozens of cards** (layout thrash). Prefer instant mount; optional short opacity only; honor `prefers-reduced-motion` |
| Nested scroll / max-height trap | Still **rejected** — fights page scroll (not “direct manipulation” of the page) |

**Verdict:** Progressive disclosure remains the right pattern. Two plan changes after the skills pass:

1. Scope to **mobile only** (was “all breakpoints”).
2. Preview **3** roles (was 5) — tall cards; aligns with cities preview and keeps ~one viewport of roles.
3. Add **Show less → scroll into view** and **no list-height animation**.

## Chosen behavior

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  card[CompanyCard positions visible]
  mobile{viewport max-width 720px?}
  full[Render all openJobs]
  over{openJobs length greater than 3?}
  preview[Render first 3 JobCards]
  btnMore["Show N more roles"]
  allRoles[Render all JobCards]
  btnLess[Show less]
  scrollBack[scrollIntoView company card]

  card --> mobile
  mobile -->|no| full
  mobile -->|yes| over
  over -->|no| full
  over -->|yes preview| preview --> btnMore
  btnMore -->|tap| allRoles --> btnLess
  btnLess -->|tap| preview --> scrollBack
```

**Defaults:**
- `ROLE_PREVIEW_LIMIT = 3`
- Mobile gate: `window.matchMedia("(max-width: 720px)")` (same breakpoint as existing board mobile CSS)
- Sort unchanged (`sortJobsForDisplay`: ATS → pinned → rest) so the preview is the most useful 3
- Rejected / not-for-me sections: same 3-role preview when those toggles are on (secondary lists, same trap)
- Expanded state: local React state only (resets on remount / page change)
- Existing company “Hide positions” untouched

## Implementation

Primary file: [`frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx). Light CSS in [`relocation_jobs/static/styles.css`](relocation_jobs/static/styles.css).

1. `ROLE_PREVIEW_LIMIT = 3` + `rolesExpanded` state + `isMobile` from `matchMedia("(max-width: 720px)")` with a resize/listener (or CSS-hidden desktop duplicate avoided — prefer one list + JS gate).
2. When `isMobile && !rolesExpanded && openJobs.length > 3`, render `openJobs.slice(0, 3)` plus a full-width `.expand-roles-btn` (`Show ${n} more roles`). When expanded, render all + `Show less`.
3. On Show less: set `rolesExpanded` false, then `cardRef.scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest", behavior: prefers-reduced-motion ? "auto" : "smooth" })`.
4. CSS: touch-friendly `min-height: 2.5rem` under existing `@media (max-width: 720px)`; `:active { transform: scale(0.97) }` with short ease-out (~160ms); no `height` transition on `.positions`.
5. Optional (only if it does not fight sticky toolbar): sticky `.company-header` on mobile while expanded — improves “Where am I?”; skip if awkward with existing chrome.
6. `cd frontend && npm run build`.

No API / board pagination changes.

## Out of scope

- Desktop role capping
- Flattening the board away from company grouping
- Persisting expand state in `localStorage`
- Redesigning `position-card` density (separate pass)

Skills did change the plan in three places:

1. **Mobile-only** — Apple Flexibility: phone = quick scan; desktop = deep workflow. Cap roles under `720px`; desktop keeps the full list.
2. **Preview 3, not 5** — position cards are tall; match `CITY_PREVIEW_LIMIT` and keep roughly one viewport of roles.
3. **Craft details from the skills** — specific copy (`Show N more roles`); on Show less, scroll the company back into view (wayfinding); `:active` press scale; **no height animation** of the list (Emil: only transform/opacity; animating dozens of cards would thrash layout).

content.text:
### What’s wrong (ux-skills lens)

Against [`.claude/ux-skills/apple-design`](.claude/ux-skills/apple-design/SKILL.md) — especially **Simplicity**, **Purpose**, **Flexibility**, **Grouping**, and **Wayfinding**:

**Company card** ([`CompanyCard.jsx`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx))
- Primary job (scan company → open roles) competes with admin chrome: Rename, Edit URL, Remove, Fetch, Awaiting response, Show rejected / not-for-me, Hide positions, duplicate country badges.
- On mobile the toolbar becomes a 2-column **button grid** — everything looks equally important (Simplicity fails: common path isn’t first).
- Status is repeated: badges in the side column *and* active buttons in the toolbar (Familiarity / mapping is weak).

**Position card** ([`position-card.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/position-card.js) `_htmlOpen`)
- One open role always surfaces ~6 actions: I applied, Got rejection, Looking to apply, Saw before, Waiting referral, Hide… — plus pin + ATS.
- Status is duplicated as **badge + button** (applied / looking / seen).
- Badge row can stack visa, applied, referral, looking, seen, CV badges, date — dense noise before the title hierarchy settles.
- Tall cards amplify the scroll problem we just fixed with role preview.

**Emil craft notes:** press feedback and hierarchy are uneven; secondary admin actions look like primary workflow actions.

---

### How I’d make it better (recommendation)

**Mobile-first progressive disclosure** — same philosophy as the roles “Show N more” fix:

| Layer | Company | Position |
|-------|---------|----------|
| Always visible | Name, locations, country, role count, 1–2 status chips | Title, city, visa (if any), one status chip, date |
| Primary actions | Collapse / expand roles; open workspace | Open JD (title link); **I applied** / **Hide** |
| One level deeper | “⋯” menu: Rename, Edit URL, Fetch, Remove, Awaiting, Show rejected/NFM | “⋯” or overflow: Looking, Saw before, Referral, Reapply/Restore, Pin, ATS |

Desktop keeps more visible (deep workflow), mobile shows the common path first.

---

**How deep should this go?**

1. **Mobile hierarchy pass** — tuck secondary/admin behind menus; clearer primary actions; keep all capabilities  
2. **Full IA rethink** — redesign status model + action groups for desktop and mobile  
3. **Diagnose only** — recommendations now, no implementation plan yet  

Reply with `1`, `2`, or `3` and I’ll lock the plan to that.

input.plan:
# Company + position card IA redesign

## Diagnosis (ux-skills)

Apple **Simplicity / Purpose / Grouping** fail today: every admin and status control competes at the same visual weight. Status is shown twice (badge + active button). Mobile turns the company toolbar into a 2-column button grid ([`styles.css` ~5293](relocation_jobs/static/styles.css)). Position open cards always dump ~6 actions ([`position-card.js` `_htmlOpen`](relocation_jobs/static/js/position-card.js)).

Backend rules stay unchanged ([`docs/reference/business-rules.md`](docs/reference/business-rules.md)): three buckets, apply clears looking, reject does not clear applied, etc. This is a **presentation** redesign.

## New status model (UI)

Separate three layers that the UI currently conflates:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  bucket[Bucket: open / rejected / not_for_me]
  pipeline[Pipeline status chip]
  annotations[Annotations]

  bucket --> cardVariant[Card variant + primary actions]
  pipeline --> oneChip["At most one primary chip"]
  annotations --> meta["Seen / pin / ATS / CV / visa / date"]
```

**Pipeline priority for open roles** (one primary chip; chip *is* the control when active — no duplicate badge):

1. Applied (with date) — tap clears apply  
2. Else Looking to apply — tap clears  
3. Else Waiting referral — tap opens referral editor  
4. Else none (idle)

**Annotations** (never compete with title): visa, city, relative date, seen (dim card only — no “Saw before” badge when idle), ATS ring, pin icon, CV doc links.

**Rejected / not-for-me variants:** keep bucket-specific primary action only (`Reapply` / `Restore` + reason chip). No open-pipeline action row.

## Action groups

### Position (open)

| Tier | Controls | Where |
|------|----------|--------|
| Primary | Idle: **I applied** + **Hide ▾**. Applied: **Applied · date** (toggle) + **Got rejection** | Always visible action row |
| Secondary | Looking, Saw before, Referral (when not already primary chip), Pin | Desktop: compact secondary row. Mobile: **⋯ More** menu |
| Meta | ATS (side), title→JD, city, visa, date, CV badges | Header / meta line |

### Company

| Tier | Controls | Where |
|------|----------|--------|
| Identity | Name→workspace, locations, country once, `N roles` | Header |
| Status | Applied count chip, Awaiting chip (chip toggles awaiting) | Header chips only — remove duplicate awaiting button when inactive? Show **Awaiting** as primary toggle in header; active state is the chip |
| Structure | Collapse chevron (replaces long “Hide positions” label on mobile) | Header trailing |
| Lists | “Rejected (n)” / “Not for me (n)” as quiet text toggles | Under meta, only when count > 0 |
| Admin | Rename, Edit URL, Fetch, Remove | **⋯** menu (desktop + mobile) |
| Careers | Careers link only | Meta; Edit URL lives in ⋯ |

## Layout targets

**Company card**

```
[Name ───────────────────── ⋯] [chevron]
locations · country · N roles
[Applied · 2] [Awaiting]     optional chips
Careers page
Rejected (3) · Not for me (1)   quiet toggles
──────── positions ────────
```

**Position card (open, idle)**

```
Title                          [pin*] [ATS]
city · Visa · 2d ago
[ I applied ] [ Hide ▾ ]       primary
Looking · Saw before · Referral  secondary (desktop) / inside ⋯ (mobile)
```
\*pin moves to secondary/⋯ on mobile to protect title width.

**Position card (applied)**

```
Title                          [ATS]
city · Visa · 2d ago
[ Applied · 12 Mar ] [ Got rejection ]
… secondary as above
```

## Implementation approach

Preserve existing event hooks (`data-applied`, `.applied-btn`, `.hide-reason-trigger`, company `data-*` buttons, etc.) so [`events.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/events.js) keeps working — change **structure and CSS**, not API contracts.

1. **[`frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx)**  
   - Restructure header: identity + trailing ⋯ + collapse.  
   - Move Rename / Edit URL / Fetch / Remove into a small overflow menu (native `<details>`/`<menu>` or button+popover matching existing filter popover pattern).  
   - Status as chips; quiet list toggles; drop duplicate country badge in side column.  
   - Keep role preview (`ROLE_PREVIEW_LIMIT`) as already shipped.

2. **[`relocation_jobs/static/js/position-card.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/position-card.js)**  
   - Rewrite `_htmlOpen` / rejected / not-for-me templates to the tiers above.  
   - Remove badge when the matching primary button is showing the same state.  
   - Add `.position-actions-primary` / `.position-actions-secondary` / `.position-more` (mobile overflow).  
   - `seen` → visual dim only when not also shown as an active secondary control.

3. **[`relocation_jobs/static/styles.css`](relocation_jobs/static/styles.css)**  
   - New hierarchy: title dominates; meta muted; primary actions ≥44px touch on mobile; secondary quieter.  
   - Kill mobile 2-column admin grid for company toolbar.  
   - Press feedback (`:active` scale ~0.97) on primary actions; `prefers-reduced-motion` respected.  
   - Desktop (≥721px): secondary row visible. Mobile: secondary inside ⋯.

4. **Wire overflow menus**  
   - Company: React state or lightweight popover (mirror filter/user menu patterns in [`index.html`](relocation_jobs/static/index.html) / events).  
   - Position: HTML in `position-card` + click handlers in `events.js` (open/close more menu; stopPropagation). Reuse existing referral/hide popover stacking discipline.

5. **Rebuild** `cd frontend && npm run build`. Smoke-check board at ≤720px and desktop: apply, unapply, reject, hide reason, referral, pin, ATS, collapse, fetch, remove.

## Out of scope

- Changing DB / bucket rules or filter semantics

# Company + position card IA redesign

## Diagnosis

Apple **Simplicity / Purpose / Grouping** and ui-ux-pro-max **Touch / Hierarchy** fail today: admin and status controls compete at equal weight; status is duplicated (badge + button); mobile company toolbar is a 2-column button grid; open positions dump ~6 actions.

Backend rules unchanged ([`docs/reference/business-rules.md`](docs/reference/business-rules.md)). Presentation-only redesign.

## ui-ux-pro-max review (validates + corrects the plan)

Ran [`.cursor/skills/ui-ux-pro-max`](.cursor/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/SKILL.md):

```bash
python3 scripts/search.py "job board productivity dashboard dense tracking tool dark" \
  --design-system --density 8 --motion 3 --variance 3 -p "Relocation Jobs Panel"
# + --domain ux for touch / overflow / a11y / confirmation
```

| Skill finding | Verdict on prior plan |
|---------------|----------------------|
| **Density 8** (dashboard spacing) | Keep — denser cards, tighter meta; 4/8dp rhythm |
| **Motion 3 / Variance 3** (subtle, minimal) | Keep — no height choreography; micro-feedback only 150–300ms |
| **Primary CTA visible; anti-pattern “Hidden filters”** | Keep primary triage visible (Apply / Hide); only secondary/admin in ⋯ |
| **No gesture-only actions** (Critical) | Keep — all actions are buttons, not swipe-only |
| **Touch ≥44×44 + ≥8px gap** (High) | Strengthen — primary row gap ≥8px; icon hit areas padded |
| **Hover ≠ tap** (High) | Strengthen — secondary must not depend on hover; mobile ⋯ always visible |
| **Icon-only need `aria-label`** (High) | Add — ⋯, chevron, pin |
| **Confirm destructive** (High) | **Add** — Remove company needs confirm dialog (plan missed this) |
| **Loading buttons** (High) | Keep — Fetch disabled + “Fetching…” while running |
| **Z-index scale / stacking** (High) | **Add** — menus float like hide-reason; don’t clip with `overflow: hidden` on cards |
| **Focus rings + keyboard Escape** (High) | **Add** — visible focus; Escape closes ⋯ |
| **Color not only status cue** (High) | Keep — chips always have text labels |
| **Press state must not shift layout** (Visual Quality) | **Correct** — prefer opacity/background press, not unbounded `scale(0.97)` that jitters neighbors |
| **No emoji as icons** | **Correct** — SVG dots-three / caret, not literal “⋯” character as the only glyph (text label OK as fallback with aria) |
| **Design-system light-blue + Inter palette** | **Reject for this pass** — panel already has dark tokens; do not swap brand colors/fonts mid-board. Use existing CSS variables |
| **Virtualize lists >100** | Out of scope — company pagination + role preview already bound DOM |

**Net:** IA (primary / secondary / admin) stands. Plan upgrades: Remove confirmation, floating menus + a11y, opacity press feedback, SVG icons, keep existing theme.

## New status model (UI)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  bucket[Bucket: open / rejected / not_for_me]
  pipeline[Pipeline status]
  annotations[Annotations]

  bucket --> cardVariant[Card variant + primary actions]
  pipeline --> oneControl["One primary control shows state"]
  annotations --> meta["visa / city / date / ATS / pin / CV"]
```

**Pipeline priority (open)** — active state *is* the control (no duplicate badge):

1. Applied · date — tap clears  
2. Else Looking — tap clears  
3. Else Waiting referral — tap opens editor  
4. Else idle  

**Annotations:** visa, city, relative date, seen (dim card only), ATS, pin, CV links. Color + text on every status chip.

**Rejected / not-for-me:** bucket primary only (`Reapply` / `Restore` + reason).

## Action groups

### Position (open)

| Tier | Controls | Where |
|------|----------|--------|
| Primary | Idle: **I applied** + **Hide ▾**. Applied: **Applied · date** + **Got rejection** | Always visible; ≥44px height; ≥8px gap |
| Secondary | Looking, Saw before, Referral (if not primary), Pin | Desktop: secondary row. Mobile: **More** menu (SVG) |
| Meta | ATS, title→JD, city · visa · date, CV | Header / meta line |

### Company

| Tier | Controls | Where |
|------|----------|--------|
| Identity | Name→workspace, locations, country once, `N roles` | Header |
| Status | Applied count chip; **Awaiting** toggle-as-chip | Header |
| Structure | Collapse chevron (`aria-label`, `aria-expanded`) | Trailing |
| Lists | Rejected (n) / Not for me (n) quiet text | Under meta when count > 0 |
| Admin | Rename, Edit URL, Fetch, **Remove** (confirm) | **More** menu |
| Careers | Link only; Edit URL in More | Meta |

## Layout targets

**Company**

```
[Name ──────────────── More] [chevron]
locations · country · N roles
[Applied · 2] [Awaiting]
Careers page
Rejected (3) · Not for me (1)
──────── positions ────────
```

**Position (idle / applied)** — as before; pin in More on mobile.

## Implementation

Preserve event hooks (`.applied-btn`, `data-*`, hide-reason, etc.) — structure/CSS first.

1. **[`frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx`](frontend/src/CompanyCard.jsx)** — header IA; More menu; Remove → existing confirm pattern or small modal (“Remove this company?”); keep role preview.
2. **[`relocation_jobs/static/js/position-card.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/position-card.js)** — primary/secondary/more tiers; drop duplicate badges; SVG More + `aria-expanded`.
3. **[`relocation_jobs/static/styles.css`](relocation_jobs/static/styles.css)** — dense rhythm; kill mobile admin grid; touch sizes/gaps; `:active` via opacity/bg; `:focus-visible` rings; `touch-action: manipulation`; `@media (prefers-reduced-motion)`; existing tokens only.
4. **Menus** — float/portal like hide-reason ([`events.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/events.js)); z-index from existing scale; Escape + click-outside; no card `overflow: hidden` clipping menus.
5. **Build + smoke** — 375 / 768 / desktop; keyboard tab through More; remove confirm; apply/reject/hide/referral/pin/ATS/fetch.

## Out of scope

- New color palette / Inter font swap from design-system output  
- DB / filter semantics  
- List virtualization  
- Homepage  
- ATS widget internals  

## Success criteria

- Idle open role: ≤2 primary buttons on mobile, ≥8px apart, ≥44px tall  
- No badge+button duplication for the same state  
- Admin behind More; Remove confirms  
- Menus never clipped; Escape closes; icon buttons labeled  
- All tracking actions reachable in ≤1 extra tap  
- Existing theme tokens unchanged

"""Throwaway: capture mobile screenshots of the local panel board for UX review."""
import os
import pathlib
import re

from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

BASE = "http://127.0.0.1:5051"
OUT = pathlib.Path("/tmp/panel_shots")
OUT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)


def creds():
    env = pathlib.Path(".env").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    user = re.search(r"^PANEL_ADMIN_USER=(.*)$", env, re.M)
    pw = re.search(r"^PANEL_ADMIN_PASSWORD=(.*)$", env, re.M)
    return (user.group(1).strip() if user else "admin"), (pw.group(1).strip() if pw else "")


def main():
    user, pw = creds()
    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch()
        ctx = browser.new_context(
            viewport={"width": 390, "height": 844},
            device_scale_factor=2,
            is_mobile=True,
            has_touch=True,
            user_agent="Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 17_0 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15",
        )
        page = ctx.new_page()
        # authenticate via API so we land straight on the board
        r = page.request.post(f"{BASE}/api/auth/login", data={"username": user, "password": pw})
        print("login", r.status)
        page.goto(f"{BASE}/panel", wait_until="networkidle")
        page.wait_for_timeout(2500)
        page.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "01_board_top.png"))

        card = page.query_selector("article.company-card")
        if card:
            card.scroll_into_view_if_needed()
            page.wait_for_timeout(400)
            card.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "02_company_card.png"))
        # a position card
        pos = page.query_selector("position-card .position-card")
        if pos:
            pos.scroll_into_view_if_needed()
            page.wait_for_timeout(400)
            pos.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "03_position_card.png"))
            # open the mobile "More" secondary actions
            more = page.query_selector("position-card .position-more-btn")
            if more:
                more.click()
                page.wait_for_timeout(300)
                pos.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "04_position_more_open.png"))
        # open a company More menu
        summary = page.query_selector("details.company-more > summary")
        if summary:
            summary.click()
            page.wait_for_timeout(300)
            card = page.query_selector("article.company-card")
            if card:
                card.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "05_company_more_open.png"))
        # full page tall shot
        page.screenshot(path=str(OUT / "06_board_full.png"), full_page=True)
        print("done", sorted(x.name for x in OUT.glob("*.png")))
        browser.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
Copy-paste this into a new agent chat:

---

## Prompt: Hallmark redesign — whole site (Cold Snap structure + brand orange)

### Goal
Refactor the **entire user-facing panel UI** (and related shared tokens) so it uses **Hallmark Carnival / Cold Snap structure** while **preserving Kuchup brand orange** as the primary accent. Do **not** invent a new product IA, routes, or business logic. Visual/interaction layer only.

Local panel: `http://127.0.0.1:5051/panel` with `PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=1`.

### Mandatory skills (read and follow in this order)

1. **`.claude/skills/getting-started/SKILL.md`**
2. **`.claude/skills/collaboration-style/SKILL.md`** — ask before structural/file-deletion decisions; reason first.
3. **`.claude/skills/engineering-standards/SKILL.md`** — no lazy imports; no drive-by refactors; don’t commit unless asked.
4. **`.claude/skills/codebase-memory/SKILL.md`** + **`codebase-memory-mcp`** — graph/search before broad Grep/file reads.
5. **`.agents/skills/hallmark/SKILL.md`** then **`hallmark redesign`** via **`.agents/skills/hallmark/references/verbs/redesign.md`**
6. **`.agents/skills/hallmark/references/themes/carnival.md`** — Cold Snap is Drop 01 (default). Use its **structure and rhythm**, not a blind mustard/oxblood swap for brand.
7. Load Hallmark refs as needed: `typography.md`, `color.md`, `layout-and-space.md`, `motion.md`, `microinteractions.md`, `interaction-and-states.md`, `responsive.md`, `anti-patterns.md`, `slop-test.md`, `copy.md`
8. **`.cursor/skills/brand/SKILL.md`** — keep brand identity coherent; orange is non-negotiable.
9. Optional validators: **`.cursor/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/SKILL.md`**, **`.claude/ux-skills/apple-design/SKILL.md`**, **`.claude/ux-skills/emil-design-eng/SKILL.md`** for craft checks (touch targets, press feedback, hierarchy).

### Brand orange (must preserve)

Source of truth today: `relocation_jobs/static/design-tokens.css`

- Primary: `#ff6b35` (`--accent-primary`)
- Hover: `#ff7f52`
- Soft: `rgba(255, 107, 53, 0.14)`
- On-accent text: `#0a0e27` (`--text-on-accent`) — keep WCAG AA on orange fills

**Rule:** Cold Snap’s mustard (`oklch(86% 0.18 95)`) and oxblood (`oklch(40% 0.21 25)`) are **reference roles**, not locked hexes. Remap:

| Cold Snap role | Site mapping |
|---|---|
| Accent (mustard) | Brand orange `#ff6b35` (primary CTA / highlight fills) |
| Accent-2 / rules / ink-weight | Deep aubergine/ink OR a darker orange-adjacent ink — **not** purple-cyan neon |
| Paper | Warm tinted light paper (Cold Snap cream family) **or** a deliberate dark Cold Snap–adapted paper if keeping dark mode — pick one system and stick to it |
| Ink | Deep aubergine / near-black with warm chroma |

Do **not** keep Night Flight’s multi-accent carnival (blue + purple + green + orange all competing). Status colors may remain semantic, but secondary accents must be quieter than brand orange.

### What already works (do not regress)

Previous work on **company + position cards** already includes:

- Progressive disclosure of roles on mobile (`ROLE_PREVIEW_LIMIT`)
- Action hierarchy: primary / secondary / overflow menus
- Pin as header utility (not buried in action grids)
- Flat position rows (no card-in-card)
- Cold Snap **structure**: 2px rules, tight radius, hard-offset shadows, heavier headings
- Scoped Cold Snap palette on cards only (temporary) in `styles.css`

**Your job:** lift this from “cards only” to **whole site**, with orange as brand accent, via tokens — not one-off card overrides forever.

### Scope

**In scope**
- Shared tokens: `relocation_jobs/static/design-tokens.css` (+ any homepage tokens that share Night Flight)
- Panel chrome: header, filters, toolbar, dialogs, buttons, badges, forms
- Company/position cards (unify under global tokens; remove temporary card-only palette forks where possible)
- Rebuild frontend: `cd frontend && npm run build`
- Bump cache query params in `relocation_jobs/static/index.html`

**Out of scope unless user confirms**
- Deleting routes/components
- Changing job-state business rules
- Deploying to EC2 / kuchup.com
- Rewriting product copy

If homepage (`homepage/`) shares tokens/brand, include it in the same token pass; ask once if homepage styling diverges.

### Hallmark process requirements

1. Pre-flight scan existing tokens/fonts/motion.
2. Multi-page/app flow from `redesign.md`: propose a locked **`design.md`** (genre, theme family, typography, spacing, motion, CTA voice) **and wait for confirmation** before rewriting everything.
3. Genre: treat as **app surface** — prefer **modern-minimal / technical tone** with Carnival Cold Snap **poster structure** (rules, offset shadows, weight), not carnival marketing loudness across every screen.
4. Stamp CSS; append `.hallmark/log.json`.
5. Run responsive checks at **320 / 375 / 414 / 768**.
6. Run slop-test gates relevant to app UI (no purple gradients, no Inter-as-only-stack if you can improve pairing while preserving brand, no invented metrics, locked tokens, ≥44px targets, `overflow-x: clip`).

### Implementation constraints

- Prefer token-first: change `:root` / `design-tokens.css`, then thin component CSS.
- Keep React/`position-card` event handlers and APIs intact.
- No secrets in docs; do not commit unless asked.
- After build, verify served assets at `127.0.0.1:5051` include new `?v=` and markers; hard-refresh if needed.

### Success criteria

- Whole panel feels like one Cold Snap–structured system.
- Brand orange is the clear primary accent (CTAs, focus, key highlights).
- Cards remain usable on mobile (no nested cards, consistent control heights, pin separate).
- Contrast passes on light/dark choice you commit to.
- Preview screenshots at 375 and 768 before declaring done.

### First message expected from the agent

1. Skills loaded list  
2. Pre-flight findings (tokens/fonts)  
3. Proposed `design.md` summary: paper mode (light Cold Snap vs dark adaptation), orange mapping, type pairing  
4. File plan (modify / create / delete — deletions need approval)  
5. Wait for “go ahead”

---

**Note for you:** If you only want the **panel** (not the public homepage), say that in the first line when you paste. If you want **panel + homepage**, add: “Include `homepage/` marketing pages in the same token system.”
